content: For stock sync between admin and public storefront, Supabase Realtime Postgres Changes (websocket subscription to table updates) is available but adds complexity (publication config, client subscription lifecycle) that is unnecessary for an MVP at dozens-of-resellers scale with a 'few seconds, never minutes' requirement. Next.js 16's Cache Components make dynamic (uncached) rendering the default, so simply not opting the storefront page into 'use cache' means every page load/navigation hits Supabase directly for current stock -- zero staleness with no extra sync layer. Realtime subscriptions are a valid progressive enhancement later (to update an already-open tab live) but not required for launch.
